author: Clement of Rome
book: The First Epistle of Clement to the Corinthians.

classification.cautionReason: Valued early Christian text, but not part of the biblical canon for mainstream Christian denominations.

lineage: The First Epistle of Clement to the Corinthians.
We Must Adhere to Those Who Cultivate Peace, Not to Those Who Merely Pretend to Do So.

ccel.title: ANF09. The Gospel of Peter, The Diatessaron of Tatian, The Apocalypse of Peter, the Vision of Paul, The Apocalypse of the Virgin and Sedrach, The Testament of Abraham, The Acts of Xanthippe and Polyxena, The Narrative of Zosimus, The Apology of Aristides, The Epistles of Clement (complete text), Origen’s Commentary on John, Books 1–10, and Commentary on Matthew, Books 1, 2, and 10–14.

title: We Must Adhere to Those Who Cultivate Peace, Not to Those Who Merely Pretend to Do So.

verses.book: The First Epistle of Clement to the Corinthians.
verses.text: Let us cleave, therefore, to those who cultivate peace with godliness, and not to those who hypocritically profess to desire it.

verses.book: The First Epistle of Clement to the Corinthians.
verses.text: For [the Scripture] saith in a certain place, “This people honoureth me with their lips, but their heart is far from me.”

verses.book: The First Epistle of Clement to the Corinthians.
verses.text: And again: “They bless with their mouth, but curse with their heart.”

verses.book: The First Epistle of Clement to the Corinthians.
verses.text: And again it saith, “They loved Him with their month, and lied to Him with their tongue; but their heart was not right with Him, neither were they faithful in His covenant.”

verses.book: The First Epistle of Clement to the Corinthians.
verses.text: “Let the deceitful lips become silent, [and “let the Lord destroy all the lying lips,] and the boastful tongue of those who have said, Let us magnify our tongue: our lips are our own; who is lord over us?

verses.book: The First Epistle of Clement to the Corinthians.
verses.text: For the oppression of the poor, and for the sighing of the needy, will I now arise, saith the Lord: I will place him in safety; I will deal confidently with him.”
